UN PETIT ONTOLOGIE The logic of the small system Here are some exceptions to the rules that would normally be present in drawing 1. the knock: it didn’t actually happen but in my head, it did 2. essential characteristics - just leave in what you remember. you can interate to eliminate non-essential elements 3. extra stuff. ex: stuff that wasn’t in the scene, but was there in the “mind” of the scene - that helps express the “purpose” of the scene…what was really going on…for example, in this drawing, close to include all the times in the past where she let me read her diary, but she was not to read mine.

4. EXTRA ELEMENTS: I also included the light fixture (+ show…
4. The rug - imagined her perched on the rug, though she was probably not (because that’s where I would have been.

Un Petit Ontologie (game) Note: le petit ontologie can also refer to the small logic or temporary, instrumental logic suggested by, or for, a given situation. For example. - in order to justify writing this (because I needed tow rite it but id didn’t know why I told myself that I was writing it for mason even though I don’t know the intended audience appreciated the qualities, and was me at the moment (pic) something in common with Dream Logic WITE: (what’s in the ellipse): does: “missing logic, extra logic” more rules, less rules - there are some rules that are not included in the original logic, which, in our minds (my minds) looks like physics. To “extrapolate the example”: missing logic: people can turn into other people, extra: all corresponds to things in the mind, or: lighting can’t change, numbers can’t stay the same.

(Missing: Similarity to sigil)
HOW TO PLAY: the basis of the game:
identify a situation that you would like to “ontologize” There is something - some heart, or some (?) that remains unidentified, elusive - to be caught or captured.
@@PB@@

let this memory (the memory) permeate your periphery. Let it soak in, until only the essential elements remain. Now - draw the scene as needed, only leave out everything that is redundant at least metaphorically. As you draw the scene, consider these “licenses,” which afford extra opportunities. How is this night different than all other nights: 1. A normal drawing is a representational, and meant to approximate the scene as a photo to, so that, looking at the scene is a similar experience to viewing the scene with your eyes. On this night, “the night” is meant to approximate a photo of your mind, so that: the essential elements come forward, and what is important in your memory becomes prominent, also in the drawing. To take it a step further, non- prominent elements can be reduced to barely nothing, or nothing.

2. on other nights, the challenges of perspective must be fced, and defeated, through an understanding of “physics.” On this night, the challenges of perspective must be avoided, and the oddities of non-perspective must be accepted. 1. On other nights, only the things in the scene are allowed to be in the scene. On this night, included are even things that are allowed to be in the scene that also: are imagined, were imagined in the scene. For example: the knock, and her sitting on the rug (which is not definite, only imagined) as definite) @@PB@@

similar to a diagram (or map? or something) - extra elements - missing logic - emphasis, or strict addition of new elements that are tied to the scene, but by memory alone - “Make sure to include everything.” If it’s not there, no one will know about it.” (And so, in the example given, A “HISTORY” is added: of the girl getting to read the other girl’s diaries a number of times before this event. Added context is the diagram-like “Benjamin” which adds to the intention of the situation. In a very sigil like moment, the history, abbreviated (pic) is summarized into these three pairs of eyes, which are directly connected to the notebook pages.

These are the hands in the other drawing, to depict the parallel. The choice to depict single rather than double pages (spreads) adds to the directness (”of the viewing experience”) - there is no extraneous allusion to “bookhood” that breaks the single line between viewing experience, and viewed subject. These are similar to symbols, and can be iterated on towards simplification, and their proportions can be changed. One might imagine, even, these being used in a different context, to signify the same thing, as they have developed an element of universality.
@@PB@@

And lastly, to head off an imminent confusion, 3. On other nights, the discrepancy between apparent effort respectively, in each duplicated element, must be minimized. On this night such a discrepancy is tolerated, if not encouraged, if and only if it mimics the relative size as it remains in memory. In this example, the rug has details which trump other elements in the scene (as does the wood grain - there was no other way to depict wood). And , as explained in the logical. version of the drawing itself, the rug

2 exceptions to the rule(s): SOME THINGS can be depicted as a photograph, if they appear that way in memory, for example, the fluffs on the rug are designed to make the rug look more like the rug in question, to prevent any ambiguity as to which rug it was.

TWO EXAMPLES collusion/obfuration: - when I am biting something, ex. the granola, and then I am trying to see if I should still bite it, with my teeth: to click them but i can’t because the biting is taking place in the mouth, where the granola is. 1. The screen on my phone has a dandelion, where I can see the line go by (pic) and this is the other line that has to go by

2. When something obscures another, like the two scars on my arm, which are in the same place: “if I were to describe, where I got each of them, someone would be confused. How are there two descriptions for the same scar. And I couldn’t pull up my skin. Fourth example: stickers - when the stickers aren’t representational or symbolic, and then they have to become, are treated as: vice versa. there is meaning where there is not. There is there is extra meaning.

INTRODUCTION Traditionally, a “petite ontology” refers to something that has, within it, a systematic structure, in that its existence implies the existence of a set of rules - a system. Another understanding of this phenomenon - a simplified, or mechanistic - “reduced” understanding, is that there are at least two elements, in at least one instance, which means something, and this informs the meaning of the second, or linked item.
